warnings.message: Both tasks have acceptance_criteria post_conditions at WARN severity, not FAIL. The core proof ('A test can assert toInventoryItem returns lowStockThreshold from the source row.') would not block shipment if unmet — only
Test task acceptance_criteria post_condition is WARN. If the test does not actually assert lowStockThreshold, the gate would not block.

Test task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010201 has empty depends_on despite logically requiring the service implementation (DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101) to exist first. Parallel execution could cause the test to fail against an unmodified
demand.approval.approved is true and approved_by is 'user', but approved_at is null. Approval traceability is incomplete.
base_commit is 'abcdef0', a placeholder. An executing agent cannot verify it is building on the correct starting state.

Task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101 has only 1 FAIL-severity post_condition (target_file_modified). The acceptance_criteria post_condition is WARN, not FAIL. An executable PRD should have at least one behavioral FAIL post_condit

The PRD says 'low-stock badge on affected SKUs' but never defines what 'low-stock' means (e.g., quantity < 5, or a configurable threshold). The target file src/pages/inventory-list.tsx currently renders raw quantities wi

5 of 6 resolver slots resolved to unknown/custom (platform_adapter, stack_adapter, component_adapter, design_reference_pack, quality_rule_pack). Only acceptance_adapter resolved (local-browser). The agent will lack proje

The target file is a plain JSX function with no types, no imports, and no component library. The PRD does not specify whether to use CSS modules, Tailwind, inline styles, or a component library for the badge styling. An
Demand approval has approved_by='user' but approved_at=null and reason=''. While approved=true, the missing timestamp weakens the audit trail for a demand that requires demand_contract.

Both tasks have acceptance_criteria post_conditions at severity WARN rather than FAIL. A coding agent could satisfy the target_file_modified gate without actually satisfying the acceptance criteria. Consider promoting ac
demand.approval.approved_at is null and approval_reason is empty. Approval exists but lacks timestamp and reason traceability.

Session handoff paths reference tasks/DEMAND-REQ-001-*/{session.json,handoff.md,evidence.jsonl} directories that do not exist on disk. These are expected to be created by the runner at execution time, but a naive agent r
Source question IDs not propagated to scenarios or tasks; low-impact since no executable PRD was produced.
Demand approval.approved_at is null despite approved=true; traceability is weakened.
Acceptance criteria post_conditions use severity WARN rather than FAIL on both tasks; a coding agent could skip proof validation without failing the gate.

Task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101 has acceptance_criteria post-condition POST-DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101-SCENARIO-1 at severity WARN, not FAIL. A coding agent can pass the gate by modifying the target file without correctly implem
Acceptance criteria references 'a SKU below threshold' but no concrete stock threshold value is defined anywhere in the PRD, scenarios, or handoff. A coding agent must guess the threshold value.
demand.approval.approved_at is null and approval.reason is empty — approval is structurally present (approved_by: "user") but lacks timestamp and rationale.
base_commit is "abcdef0", a placeholder value. A coding agent cannot use this for safe rollback or diff verification.

Design DES-REQ-001 text is generic template language ("Implement REQ-001 according to approved demand CONTEXT.md and ROADMAP.md"). Task-level handoff details compensate, but the design artifact itself carries no concrete

handoff_completeness dimension is listed as blocked (score 62) but its block_threshold is 60 — score exceeds threshold yet dimension status is 'blocked'. Individual critical-check failures may be overriding the aggregate

Readiness is L1, not L3. The blocker correctly identifies that execution cannot proceed without bounded scope, but the readiness gap (L1 vs L3) is a secondary signal that could be more explicit in next_actions.

Both tasks have acceptance_criteria post_conditions set to severity WARN instead of FAIL. The only FAIL-gated post_condition is target_file_modified, which only verifies the file was touched — not that the requirement wa
Same issue for the test task — acceptance_criteria enforcement is WARN-only, so the test task could ship without actually asserting toInventoryItem returns lowStockThreshold.
Demand approval has approved: true and approved_by: 'user' but approved_at is null. No evidence of when approval was granted.
demand.approval.approved is true but approved_at is null — the approval timestamp was never recorded, which weakens the audit trail for a fully executable PRD.

Task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101 has only one FAIL-severity postcondition (target_file_modified). The acceptance_criteria postcondition is WARN severity, so a coding agent could technically ship without satisfying the badge v

Platform, stack, component, design-reference, and quality-rule adapters all resolved to unknown/custom. Only the acceptance_adapter (local-browser) is concrete. A fresh coding agent lacks project-specific framework guida

Both tasks have acceptance_criteria post_conditions with severity WARN instead of FAIL. Behavioral acceptance ('A regression test can call validateOrder with quantity -1 and observe an error.') will not block shipment if
Test task acceptance_criteria post_condition is WARN, not FAIL. The behavioral gate for the regression test is non-blocking.

Test task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010201 has depends_on: [] but logically depends on implementation task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101. Executing out of order would leave the agent writing a test for a function that hasn't been modifie

Test task read_first and readonly_files only reference src/api/orders.test.ts. The agent needs src/api/orders.ts (the implementation file) as a readonly reference to write a correct test against the validateOrder interfa

Both tasks have behavioral acceptance post_conditions at severity WARN, not FAIL. Only structural target_file_modified gates are FAIL. An agent could skip behavioral verification and still pass gates.
Test task behavioral acceptance post_condition is WARN severity. The test verification proof should be a FAIL gate to prevent a false-green execution.
Test task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010201 does not depend on implementation task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101. Execution order is uncontrolled.
Demand approval.approved_at is null despite approved=true. Approval lacks a timestamp.
Demand approval.approved_at is null — approval is flagged true but lacks a timestamp, weakening the audit trail.

Acceptance-criteria post-condition (POST-DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101-SCENARIO-1) is severity WARN not FAIL. A coding agent could ship without satisfying the acceptance criteria and the gate would not block.

Session state path referenced in handoff (.yolo/demand/DEMAND-0017-ui-badge-with-adapter/tasks/DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101/session.json) does not exist on disk. A fresh agent would need this file created by the runner.
The PRD says 'low-stock badge on affected SKUs' but never defines the stock threshold (e.g., qty <= 5). A coding agent must guess or pick an arbitrary number.

Design DES-REQ-001 says 'Implement REQ-001 according to approved demand CONTEXT.md and ROADMAP.md' — this is procedural, not a concrete design specification. An agent has no visual/behavioral detail to implement against.

Both tasks have acceptance_criteria post_conditions with severity 'WARN' instead of 'FAIL'. Only target_file_modified is FAIL. An agent could pass by touching the file without correctly implementing negative-quantity val
Test task has acceptance_criteria post_condition as WARN. The behavioral gate (call validateOrder with quantity -1 and observe an error) is not enforced as a hard failure.

Test task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010201 has depends_on:[] but logically depends on implementation task DEMAND-REQ-001-0010101. Running in parallel could cause the test to fail against non-existent validation logic.
demand.approval.approved is true but approved_at is null and reason is empty. Traceability of when/why approval was granted is lost.
